title: Certificates/Forms Assistance * Immigration Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Certificates/Forms Assistance * Immigration Issues 5 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Disabilities Issues Immigration Issues Transgender Individuals Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION FAMILY EMPOWERMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal clinics, assistance with the citizenship process, housing support and advocacy, education support and advocacy, tax assistance including Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN) application and renewal, referrals to jobs, translation and interpretation assistance, and information and referral to outside community resources. 2680 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 110 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 10am-5pm, Tuesday 10am-7pm Email (541) 382-4366 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION PRINEVILLE FAMILY EMPOWERMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal clinics, assistance with the citizenship process, housing support and advocacy, education support and advocacy, tax assistance including Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN) application and renewal, referrals to jobs, translation and interpretation assistance, and information and referral to outside community resources. 185 NW 4th Street Suite 1 Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 350-8910 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION PRINEVILLE WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides English tutoring, ESL, and computer classes. Also offers help with resumes, job applications, small business workshops, and related advice. 185 NW 4th Street Suite 1 Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus is on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday-Wednesday 10am-5pm or by appointment, Thursday 10am-7pm Email (541) 788-4829 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION REDMOND FAMILY EMPOWERMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal clinics, assistance with the citizenship process, housing support and advocacy, education support and advocacy, tax assistance including Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN) application and renewal, referrals to jobs, translation and interpretation assistance, and information and referral to outside community resources. 636 NW Cedar Avenue Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday 10am-7pm, Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday 10am-5pm or by appointment Email (541) 316-5456 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MEXICAN CONSULATE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Issues and renews passports and matrícula consular cards. Issues student, work, and retiree visas, and provides assistance with other documents. 1305 SW 12th Avenue Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Consulate hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm, Saturday-Sunday appointments available by request Mexitel phone hours: Monday-Friday 6am-7pm, Saturday-Sunday 7am-4pm Email (503) 274-1442 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES CITIZENSHIP AND IMMIGRATION SERVICES USCIS FEE WAIVER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a filing fee waiver process for certain forms and benefit types for applicants that clearly demonstrate inability to pay the fees. There is also a fee reduction option for Form I-912. 2675 Prosperity Avenue Fairfax, VA 20598 Eligibility: The form being filed is eligible for a fee waiver (see website for a complete list of eligible forms), AND the applicant qualifies based upon one of the following criteria: -Applicant, spouse, or the head of household currently receives a means-tested benefit such as TANF, SSI, SNAP, or Medicaid -Household income is at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines when filing (guideline list available on website) -Applicant is experiencing extreme financial hardship, including hardship from unexpected medical bills or emergencies, that prevents from paying the filing fee Hours: Not applicable (800) 375-5283 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION FAMILY EMPOWERMENT LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION FAMILY EMPOWERMENT LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION FAMILY EMPOWERMENT MEXICAN CONSULATE MEXICAN CONSULATE
